name: infrasys
documentation:
# infrasys

[![CI](https://github.com/NREL/infrasys/workflows/CI/badge.svg)](https://github.com/NREL/infrasys/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/NREL/infrasys/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/NREL/infrasys)

This package implements a data store for components and time series in support of Python-based
modeling packages. While it is designed to support teams modeling transmission and distribution
systems for electrical grids, it can be used by any package that needs to store components
(e.g., generators and buses) that have quantities (e.g., power and voltage) which may vary over
time.

The package was inspired by
[InfrastructureSystems.jl](https://github.com/NREL-Sienna/InfrastructureSystems.jl)

## Benefits
- Stores components in data structures that provide fast lookup and iteration by type and name.
- Provides extendable data models that enable validation and unit conversion through
[pint](https://pint.readthedocs.io/en/stable/).
- Manages time series data efficiently. Data is only loaded into system memory when needed by
the user application.
- Manages serialization and de-serialization of components to JSON, including automatic handling of
nested objects.
- Enables data model migration.

## Package Developer Guide
🚧

## Installation
```
$ pip install git+ssh://git@github.com/NREL/infrastructure_systems.git@main
```

## Developer installation
```
$ pip install -e ".[dev]"
```

Please install `pre-commit` so that your code is checked before making commits.
```
$ pre-commit install
```

## License
infrasys is released under a BSD 3-Clause
[License](https://github.com/NREL/infrasys/blob/main/LICENSE.txt).

infrasys was developed under software record SWR-24-42 at the National Renewable Energy Laboratory
([NREL](https://www.nrel.gov)).

modules.name: infrasys.quantities
documentation:
This module defines basic unit quantities.

To create new Quantities for a given base unit, we just need to specify the
base unit as the second argument of `ureg.check`.

modules.name: infrasys.time_series_manager
documentation: Manages time series arrays

classes.name: infrasys.time_series_manager.TimeSeriesManager

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_manager.TimeSeriesManager.metadata_store
documentation: Return the time series metadata store.

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_manager.TimeSeriesManager.storage
documentation: Return the time series storage object.

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_manager.TimeSeriesManager.add
documentation:
Store a time series array for one or more components.

Parameters
----------
time_series : TimeSeriesData
    Time series data to store.
components : Component
    Add the time series to all of these components.
user_attributes : Any
    Key/value pairs to store with the time series data. Must be JSON-serializable.

Raises
------
ISAlreadyAttached
    Raised if the variable name and user attributes match any time series already
    attached to one of the components.
ISOperationNotAllowed
    Raised if the manager was created in read-only mode.

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_manager.TimeSeriesManager.get
documentation:
Return a time series array.

Raises
------
ISNotStored
    Raised if no time series matches the inputs.
    Raised if the inputs match more than one time series.
ISOperationNotAllowed
    Raised if the inputs match more than one time series.

See Also
--------
list_time_series

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_manager.TimeSeriesManager.has_time_series
documentation: Return True if the component has time series matching the inputs.

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_manager.TimeSeriesManager.list_time_series
documentation: Return all time series that match the inputs.

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_manager.TimeSeriesManager.list_time_series_metadata
documentation: Return all time series metadata that match the inputs.

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_manager.TimeSeriesManager.remove
documentation:
Remove all time series arrays matching the inputs.

Raises
------
ISNotStored
    Raised if no time series match the inputs.
ISOperationNotAllowed
    Raised if the manager was created in read-only mode.

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_manager.TimeSeriesManager.copy
documentation:
Copy all time series from src to dst.

Parameters
----------
dst : Component
    Destination component
src : Component
    Source component
name_mapping : dict[str, str]
    Optionally map src names to different dst names.
    If provided and src has a time_series with a name not present in name_mapping, that
    time_series will not copied. If name_mapping is nothing then all time_series will be
    copied with src's names.

Notes
-----
name_mapping is currently not implemented.

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_metadata_store.TimeSeriesMetadataStore._try_get_time_series_metadata_by_full_params
documentation:
Attempt to get the metadata by using all parameters.

This will return the metadata if the user passes all user attributes that exist in the
time series metadata. This is highly advantageous in cases where one component has a large
number of time series and each metadata has user attributes. Otherwise, SQLite has to
parse the JSON values.

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_metadata_store.TimeSeriesMetadataStore._try_has_time_series_metadata_by_full_params
documentation:
Attempt to check if the metadata is stored by using all parameters. Refer to
_try_get_time_series_metadata_by_full_params for more information.
Stores time series metadata in a SQLite database.

classes.name: infrasys.cost_curves.CostCurve

params.name: Field(description="(default: natural units (MW)) The units for the x-axis of the curve")

documentation:
Direct representation of the variable operation cost of a power plant in currency.

Composed of a Value Curve that may represent input-output, incremental, or average rate
data. The default units for the x-axis are MW and can be specified with
`power_units`.

classes.name: infrasys.cost_curves.FuelCurve

params.name: Field(description="(default: natural units (MW)) The units for the x-axis of the curve")

documentation:
Representation of the variable operation cost of a power plant in terms of fuel.

Fuel units (MBTU, liters, m^3, etc.) coupled with a conversion factor between fuel and currency.
Composed of a Value Curve that may represent input-output, incremental, or average rate data.
The default units for the x-axis are MW and can be specified with `power_units`.

classes.name: infrasys.component_associations.ComponentAssociations

methods.name: infrasys.component_associations.ComponentAssociations.add
documentation:
Store an association between each component and directly attached subcomponents.

- Inspects the type of each field of each component's type. Looks for subtypes of
  Component and lists of subtypes of Component.
- Does not consider component fields that are dictionaries or other data structures.

methods.name: infrasys.component_associations.ComponentAssociations.clear
documentation: Clear all component associations.

methods.name: infrasys.component_associations.ComponentAssociations.list_child_components
documentation:
Return a list of all component UUIDS that this component composes.
For example, return the bus attached to a generator.

methods.name: infrasys.component_associations.ComponentAssociations.list_parent_components
documentation:
Return a list of all component UUIDS that compose this component.
For example, return all components connected to a bus.

methods.name: infrasys.component_associations.ComponentAssociations.remove
documentation: Delete all rows with this component.

Stores associations between components. Allows callers to quickly find components composed
by other components, such as the generator to which a bus is connected.

modules.name: infrasys.function_data
documentation: Defines models for cost functions

functions.name: infrasys.function_data.validate_piecewise_linear_x
documentation:
Validates the x data for PiecewiseLinearData class

X data is checked to ensure there is at least two values of x, which is the minimum required to
generate a cost curve, and is given in ascending order (e.g. [1, 2, 3], not [1, 3, 2]).

Parameters
----------
points : List[XYCoords]
    List of named tuples of (x,y) coordinates for cost function

Returns
-------
points : List[XYCoords]
    List of (x,y) data for cost function after successful validation.

functions.name: infrasys.function_data.validate_piecewise_step_x
documentation:
Validates the x data for PiecewiseStepData class

X data is checked to ensure there is at least two values of x, which is the minimum required to
generate a cost curve, and is given in ascending order (e.g. [1, 2, 3], not [1, 3, 2]).

Parameters
----------
x_coords : List[float]
    List of x data for cost function.

Returns
-------
x_coords : List[float]
    List of x data for cost function after successful validation.

classes.name: infrasys.function_data.XYCoords
documentation: Named tuple used to define (x,y) coordinates.

classes.name: infrasys.function_data.FunctionData
documentation: BaseClass of FunctionData

classes.name: infrasys.function_data.LinearFunctionData

params.name: Field(description="the proportional term in the represented function.")
Field(description="the constant term in the represented function.")

documentation:
Data representation for linear cost function.

Used to represent linear cost functions of the form

$$
f(x) = mx + c,
$$

where $m$ is the proportional term and $c$ is the constant term.

classes.name: infrasys.function_data.QuadraticFunctionData

params.name: Field(description="the quadratic term in the represented function.")
Field(description="the proportional term in the represented function.")
Field(description="the constant term in the represented function.")

documentation:
Data representation for quadratic cost function.

Used to represent quadratic of cost functions of the form

$$
f(x) = ax^2 + bx + c,
$$

where $a$ is the quadratic term, $b$ is the proportional term and $c$ is the
constant term.

classes.name: infrasys.function_data.PiecewiseLinearData

params.name: Field(description="list of (x,y) points that define the function.")

documentation:
Data representation for piecewise linear cost function.

Used to represent linear data as a series of points: two points define one segment, three
points define two segments, etc. The curve starts at the first point given, not the origin.
Principally used for the representation of cost functions where the points store quantities (x,
y), such as (MW, USD/h).

classes.name: infrasys.function_data.PiecewiseStepData

methods.name: infrasys.function_data.PiecewiseStepData.validate_piecewise_xy
documentation:
Method to validate the x and y data for PiecewiseStepData class

Model validator used to validate given data for the {class}`PiecewiseStepData`. Calls
`validate_piecewise_step_x` to check if `x_coords` is valid, then checks if the length of
`y_coords` is exactly one less than `x_coords`, which is necessary to define the cost
functions correctly.

params.name: Field(description="the x-coordinates of the endpoints of the segments.")

documentation:
Data representation for piecewise step cost function.

Used to represent a step function as a series of endpoint x-coordinates and segment
y-coordinates: two x-coordinates and one y-coordinate defines a single segment, three
x-coordinates and two y-coordinates define two segments, etc.

This can be useful to represent the derivative of a {class}`PiecewiseLinearData`, where the
y-coordinates of this step function represent the slopes of that piecewise linear function.
Principally used for the representation of cost functions where the points store quantities (x,
$dy/dx$), such as (MW, USD/MWh).

classes.name: infrasys.time_series_models.SingleTimeSeries

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_models.SingleTimeSeries.length
documentation: Return the length of the data.

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_models.SingleTimeSeries.check_data
documentation: Check time series data.

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_models.SingleTimeSeries.from_array
documentation:
Method of SingleTimeSeries that creates an instance from a sequence.

Parameters
----------
data
    Sequence that contains the values of the time series
initial_time
    Start time for the time series (e.g., datetime(2020,1,1))
resolution
    Resolution of the time series (e.g., 30min, 1hr)
variable_name
    Name assigned to the values of the time series (e.g., active_power)

Returns
-------
SingleTimeSeries

See Also
--------
from_time_array:  Time index implementation

Note
----
- Length of the sequence is inferred from the data.

methods.name: infrasys.time_series_models.SingleTimeSeries.from_time_array
documentation:
Create SingleTimeSeries using time_index provided.

Parameters
----------
data
    Sequence that contains the values of the time series
variable_name
    Name assigned to the values of the time series (e.g., active_power)
time_index
    Sequence that contains the index of the time series

Returns
-------
SingleTimeSeries

See Also
--------
from_array: Base implementation

Note
----
The current implementation only uses the time_index to infer the initial time and resolution.
Defines a time array with a single dimension of floats.

modules.name: infrasys.loggers
documentation: Contains logging configuration data.

functions.name: infrasys.loggers.setup_logging
documentation:
Configures logging to file and console.

Parameters
----------
filename : str | None
    log filename
level : str, optional
    change defualt level of logging.
verbose :  bool
    returns additional logging information.

modules.name: infrasys.value_curves
documentation: Defines classes for value curves using cost functions

functions.name: infrasys.value_curves.LinearCurve
documentation:
Creates a linear curve using the given proportional and constant terms.

Returns an instance of `InputOutputCurve` with the specified linear function parameters.

If no arguments are provided, both the `proportional_term` and `constant_term` default to 0.

# Parameters

proportional_term

: The slope of the linear curve. Defaults to 0.0.

constant_term

: The y-intercept of the linear curve. Defaults to 0.0.

# Returns

InputOutputCurve

: An instance of `InputOutputCurve` with a `LinearFunctionData` object based on the given parameters.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> LinearCurve()
InputOutputCurve(function_data=LinearFunctionData(proportional_term=0.0, constant_term=0.0))
```

```pycon
>>> LinearCurve(10)
InputOutputCurve(function_data=LinearFunctionData(proportional_term=10.0, constant_term=0.0))
```

```pycon
>>> LinearCurve(10, 20)
InputOutputCurve(function_data=LinearFunctionData(proportional_term=10.0, constant_term=20.0))
```

```pycon
>>> LinearCurve(proportional_term=5.0, constant_term=15.0)
InputOutputCurve(function_data=LinearFunctionData(proportional_term=5.0, constant_term=15.0))
```

classes.name: infrasys.value_curves.InputOutputCurve
documentation:
Input-output curve relating production quality to cost.

An input-output curve, directly relating the production quantity to the cost:

$$
y = f(x).
$$

Can be used, for instance, in the representation of a Cost Curve where $x$ is MW and
$y$ is currency/hr, or in the representation of a Fuel Curve where $x$ is MW and
$y$ is fuel/hr.

classes.name: infrasys.value_curves.IncrementalCurve

methods.name: infrasys.value_curves.IncrementalCurve.to_input_output
documentation:
Function to convert IncrementalCurve to InputOutputCurve

Function takes an IncrementalCurve and converts it to a corresponding
InputOutputCurve depending on the type of function_data. If the IncrementalCurve
uses LinearFunctionData, the new InputOutputCurve is created linear or quadratic data
that correspond to the integral of the original linear function. If the input uses
PiecewiseStepData, the slopes of each segment are used to calculate the corresponding
y values for each x value and used to construct PiecewiseLinearData for the InputOutputCurve.

Parameters
----------
data : IncrementalCurve
    Original IncrementalCurve for conversion.

Returns
----------
InputOutputCurve
    InputOutputCurve using either QuadraticFunctionData or PiecewiseStepData.

params.name:
Field(
        description="The value of f(x) at the least x for which the function is defined, or \
                the origin for functions with no left endpoint, used for conversion to `InputOutputCurve`"
    )

documentation:
Incremental/marginal curve to relate production quantity to cost derivative.

An incremental (or 'marginal') curve, relating the production quantity to the derivative of
cost:

..math:: y = f'(x).

Can be used, for instance, in the representation of a Cost Curve
where $x$ is MW and $y$ is currency/MWh, or in the representation of a Fuel Curve
where $x$ is MW and $y$ is fuel/MWh.

classes.name: infrasys.value_curves.AverageRateCurve

methods.name: infrasys.value_curves.AverageRateCurve.to_input_output
documentation:
Function to convert IncrementalCurve to InputOutputCurve

Function takes an AverageRateCurve and converts it to a corresponding
InputOutputCurve depending on the type of function_data. If the AverageRateCurve
uses LinearFunctionData, the new InputOutputCurve is created with either linear or quadratic
function data, depending on if the original function data is constant or linear. If the
input uses PiecewiseStepData, new y-values are calculated for each x value such that `f(x) = x*y`
and used to construct PiecewiseLinearData for the InputOutputCurve.

Parameters
----------
data : AverageRateCurve
    Original AverageRateCurve for conversion.

Returns
----------
InputOutputCurve
    InputOutputCurve using either QuadraticFunctionData or PiecewiseStepData.

params.name:
Field(
        description="The underlying `FunctionData` representation of this `ValueCurve`, or \
                only the oblique asymptote when using `LinearFunctionData`"
    )

Field(
        description="The value of f(x) at the least x for which the function is defined, or \
                the origin for functions with no left endpoint, used for conversion to `InputOutputCurve`"
    )

documentation:
Average rate curve relating production quality to average cost rate.

An average rate curve, relating the production quantity to the average cost rate from the
origin:

$$
y = f(x)/x.
$$

Can be used, for instance, in the representation of a
Cost Curve where $x$ is MW and $y$ is currency/MWh, or in the representation of a
Fuel Curve where $x$ is MW and $y$ is fuel/MWh. Typically calculated by dividing
absolute values of cost rate or fuel input rate by absolute values of electric power.

modules.name: infrasys.system
documentation: Defines a System

classes.name: infrasys.system.System

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.__init__
documentation:
Constructs a System.

# Parameters

name

: Optional system name

description

: Optional system description

auto_add_composed_components

: Set to True to automatically add composed components to the system in add_components.
  The default behavior is to raise an ISOperationNotAllowed when this condition occurs.
  This handles values that are components, such as generator.bus, and lists of
  components, such as subsystem.generators, but not any other form of nested components.

con

: Users should not pass this. De-serialization (from_json) will pass a Connection.

time_series_manager

: Users should not pass this. De-serialization (from_json) will pass a constructed
  manager.

kwargs
: Configures time series behaviors:
  : - time_series_in_memory: Defaults to true.
    - time_series_read_only: Disables add/remove of time series, defaults to false.
    - time_series_directory: Location to store time series file, defaults to the system's
      tmp directory.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> system = System(name="my_system")
>>> system2 = System(name="my_system", time_series_directory="/tmp/scratch")
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.auto_add_composed_components
documentation: Return the setting for auto_add_composed_components.

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.auto_add_composed_components
documentation: Set auto_add_composed_components.

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.to_json
documentation:
Write the contents of a system to a JSON file. Time series will be written to a
directory at the same level as filename.

# Parameters

filename

: Filename to write. If the parent directory does not exist, it will be created.

overwrite

: Set to True to overwrite the file if it already exists.

indent

: Indentation level in the JSON file. Defaults to no indentation.

data

: This is an override for packages that compose this System inside a parent System
  class. If set, it will be the outer object in the JSON file. It must not set the
  key 'system'. Packages that derive a custom instance of this class should leave this
  field unset.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> system.to_json("systems/system1.json")
INFO: Wrote system data to systems/system1.json
INFO: Copied time series data to systems/system1_time_series
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.from_json
documentation:
Deserialize a System from a JSON file. Refer to System constructor for kwargs.

# Parameters

filename

: JSON file containing the system data.

upgrade_handler

: Optional function to handle data format upgrades. Should only be set when the parent
  package composes this package. If set, it will be called before de-serialization of
  the components.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> system = System.from_json("systems/system1.json")
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.to_records
documentation:
Return a list of dictionaries of components (records) with the requested type(s) and
optionally match filter_func.

# Parameters

components:

: Component types to get as dictionaries

filter_func:

: A function to filter components. Default is None

kwargs
: Configures Pydantic model_dump behaviour
  : - exclude: List or dict of excluded fields.

# Notes

If a component type is an abstract type, all matching concrete subtypes will be included in the output.

It is only recommended to use this function on a single "concrete" types. For example, if
you have an abstract type called Generator and you create two subtypes called
ThermalGenerator and RenewableGenerator where some fields are different, if you pass the
return of System.to_records(Generator) to pandas.DataFrame.from_records, each
ThermalGenerator row will have NaN values for RenewableGenerator-specific fields.

# Examples

To get a tabular representation of a certain type you can use:
\>>> import pandas as pd
\>>> df = pd.DataFrame.from_records(System.to_records(SimpleGen))

With polars:
\>>> import polars as pl
\>>> df = pl.DataFrame(System.to_records(SimpleGen))

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.from_dict
documentation:
Deserialize a System from a dictionary.

# Parameters

data

: System data in serialized form.

time_series_parent_dir

: Directory that contains the system's time series directory.

upgrade_handler

: Optional function to handle data format upgrades. Should only be set when the parent
  package composes this package. If set, it will be called before de-serialization of
  the components.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> system = System.from_dict(data, "systems")
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.save
documentation:
Save the contents of a system and the Time series in a single directory.

By default, this method creates the user specified folder using the
`to_json` method. If user sets `zip = True`, we create the folder of
the user (if it does not exists), zip it to the same location specified
and delete the folder.

# Parameters

fpath

: Filepath to write the contents of the system.

zip

: Set to True if you want to archive to a zip file.

filename: str

: Name of the sytem to serialize. Default value: "system.json".

overwrite: bool

: Overwrites the system if it already exist on the fpath.

# Raises

FileExistsError

: Raised if the folder provided exists and the overwrite flag was not provided.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> fpath = Path("folder/subfolder/")
>>> system.save(fpath)
INFO: Wrote system data to folder/subfolder/system.json
INFO: Copied time series data to folder/subfolder/system_time_series
```

```pycon
>>> system_fname = "my_system.json"
>>> fpath = Path("folder/subfolder/")
>>> system.save(fpath, filename=system_fname, zip=True)
INFO: Wrote system data to folder/subfolder/my_system.json
INFO: Copied time series data to folder/subfolder/my_system_time_series
INFO: System archived at folder/subfolder/my_system.zip
```

# See Also

to_json: System serialization

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.add_component
documentation:
Add one component to the system.

# Parameters

component

: Component to add to the system.

# Raises

ISAlreadyAttached

: Raised if a component is already attached to a system.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> system.add_component(Bus.example())
```

# See Also

add_components

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.add_components
documentation:
Add one or more components to the system.

# Parameters

components

: Component(s) to add to the system.

# Raises

ISAlreadyAttached

: Raised if a component is already attached to a system.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> system.add_components(Bus.example(), Generator.example())
```

# See Also

add_component

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.change_component_uuid
documentation:
Change the component UUID. This is required if you copy a component and attach it to
the same system.

Parameters
----------
component : Component

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.copy_component
documentation:
Create a copy of the component. Time series data is excluded.

- The new component will have a different UUID than the original.
- The copied component will have shared references to any composed components.

The intention of this method is to provide a way to create variants of a component that
will be added to the same system. Please refer to :`deepcopy_component`: to create
copies that are suitable for addition to a different system.

# Parameters

component

: Source component

name

: Optional, if None, keep the original name.

attach

: Optional, if True, attach the new component to the system.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> gen1 = system.get_component(Generator, "gen1")
>>> gen2 = system.copy_component(gen, name="gen2")
>>> gen3 = system.copy_component(gen, name="gen3", attach=True)
```

# See Also

deepcopy_component

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.deepcopy_component
documentation:
Create a deep copy of the component and all composed components. All attributes,
including names and UUIDs, will be identical to the original. Unlike
{meth}`copy_component`, there will be no shared references to composed components.

The intention of this method is to provide a way to create variants of a component that
will be added to a different system. Please refer to :`copy_component`: to create
copies that are suitable for addition to the same system.

# Parameters

component

: Source component

# Examples

```pycon
>>> gen1 = system.get_component(Generator, "gen1")
>>> gen2 = system.deepcopy_component(gen)
```

# See Also

copy_component

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.get_component
documentation:
Return the component with the passed type and name.

# Parameters

component_type

: Type of component

name

: Name of component

# Raises

ISDuplicateNames

: Raised if more than one component match the inputs.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> system.get_component(Generator, "gen1")
```

# See Also

list_by_name

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.get_component_by_label
documentation:
Return the component with the label.

Note that this method is slower than {meth}`get_component` because the
component type cannot be looked up directly. Code that is looping over components
repeatedly should not use this method.

# Parameters

label : str

# Raises

ISNotStored

: Raised if the UUID is not stored.

ISOperationNotAllowed

: Raised if there is more than one matching component.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> component = system.get_component_by_label("Bus.bus1")
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.get_component_by_uuid
documentation:
Return the component with the input UUID.

# Parameters

uuid : UUID

# Raises

ISNotStored

: Raised if the UUID is not stored.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> uuid = UUID("714c8311-8dff-4ae2-aa2e-30779a317d42")
>>> component = system.get_component_by_uuid(uuid)
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.get_components
documentation:
Return the components with the passed type(s) and that optionally match filter_func.

# Parameters

component_type

: If component_type is an abstract type, all matching subtypes will be returned.
  The function will return all the matching `component_type` passed.

filter_func

: Optional function to filter the returned values. The function must accept a component
  as a single argument.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> for component in system.get_components(Component)
    print(component.label)
>>> names = {"bus1", "bus2", "gen1", "gen2"}
>>> for component in system.get_components(
    Component,
    filter_func=lambda x: x.name in names,
):
    print(component.label)
```

To request multiple component types:
\>>> for component in system.get_components(SimpleGenerator, SimpleBus)
print(component.label)

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.get_component_types
documentation:
Return an iterable of all component types stored in the system.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> for component_type in system.get_component_types():
print(component_type)
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.has_component
documentation: Return True if the component is attached.

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.list_child_components
documentation:
Return a list of all components that this component composes.

Parameters
----------
component: Component
component_type: Optional[Type[Component]]
    Filter the returned list to components of this type.

See Also
--------
list_parent_components

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.list_parent_components
documentation:
Return a list of all components that compose this component.

An example usage is where you need to find all components connected to a bus and the Bus
class does not contain that information. The system tracks these connections internally
and can find those components quickly.

# Parameters

component: Component
component_type: Optional\[Type[Component]\]

> Filter the returned list to components of this type.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> components = system.list_parent_components(bus)
>>> print(f"These components are connected to {bus.label}: ", " ".join(components))
```

# See Also

list_child_components

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.list_components_by_name
documentation:
Return all components that match component_type and name.

Parameters
----------
component_type : Type
name : str

Examples
--------
system.list_components_by_name(Generator, "gen1")

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.iter_all_components
documentation:
Return an iterator over all components.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> for component in system.iter_all_components()
    print(component.label)
```

# See Also

get_components

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.rebuild_component_associations
documentation:
Clear the component associations and rebuild the table. This may be necessary
if a user reassigns connected components that are part of a system.

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.remove_component
documentation:
Remove the component from the system and return it.

# Parameters

component : Component
cascade_down : bool

> If True, remove all child components if they have no other parents. Defaults to True.
> For example, if a generator has a bus, no other component holds a reference to that
> bus, and you call remove_component on that generator, the bus will get removed as well.

force

: If True, remove the component even if other components hold references to this
  component. Defaults to False.

# Raises

ISNotStored

: Raised if the component is not stored in the system.

ISOperationNotAllowed

: Raised if the other components hold references to this component and force=False.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> gen = system.get_component(Generator, "gen1")
>>> system.remove_component(gen)
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.remove_component_by_name
documentation:
Remove the component with component_type and name from the system and return it.

# Parameters

component_type : Type
name : str
cascade_down : bool

> Refer {meth}`remove_component`.

force

: Refer {meth}`remove_component`.

# Raises

ISNotStored

: Raised if the inputs do not match any components in the system.

ISOperationNotAllowed

: Raised if there is more than one component with component type and name.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> generators = system.remove_by_name(Generator, "gen1")
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.remove_component_by_uuid
documentation:
Remove the component with uuid from the system and return it.

# Parameters

uuid : UUID
cascade_down : bool

> Refer {meth}`remove_component`.

force

: Refer {meth}`remove_component`.

# Raises

ISNotStored

: Raised if the UUID is not stored in the system.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> uuid = UUID("714c8311-8dff-4ae2-aa2e-30779a317d42")
>>> generator = system.remove_component_by_uuid(uuid)
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.update_components
documentation:
Update multiple components of a given type.

# Parameters

component_type

: Type of component to update. Can be abstract.

update_func

: Function to call on each component. Must take a component as a single argument.

filter_func

: Optional function to filter the components to update. Must take a component as a
  single argument.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> system.update_components(Generator, lambda x: x.active_power *= 10)
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.add_time_series
documentation:
Store a time series array for one or more components.

# Parameters

time_series

: Time series data to store.

components

: Add the time series to all of these components.

user_attributes

: Key/value pairs to store with the time series data. Must be JSON-serializable.

# Raises

ISAlreadyAttached

: Raised if the variable name and user attributes match any time series already
  attached to one of the components.

ISOperationNotAllowed

: Raised if the manager was created in read-only mode.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> gen1 = system.get_component(Generator, "gen1")
>>> gen2 = system.get_component(Generator, "gen2")
>>> ts = SingleTimeSeries.from_array(
    data=[0.86, 0.78, 0.81, 0.85, 0.79],
    variable_name="active_power",
    start_time=datetime(year=2030, month=1, day=1),
    resolution=timedelta(hours=1),
)
>>> system.add_time_series(ts, gen1, gen2)
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.copy_time_series
documentation:
Copy all time series from src to dst.

# Parameters

dst

: Destination component

src

: Source component

name_mapping

: Optionally map src names to different dst names.
  If provided and src has a time_series with a name not present in name_mapping, that
  time_series will not copied. If name_mapping is nothing then all time_series will be
  copied with src's names.

# Notes

name_mapping is currently not implemented.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> gen1 = system.get_component(Generator, "gen1")
>>> gen2 = system.get_component(Generator, "gen2")
>>> system.copy_time_series(gen1, gen2)
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.get_time_series
documentation:
Return a time series array.

# Parameters

component

: Component to which the time series must be attached.

variable_name

: Optional, search for time series with this name.

time_series_type

: Optional, search for time series with this type.

start_time

: If not None, take a slice of the time series starting at this time.

length

: If not None, take a slice of the time series with this length.

user_attributes

: Optional, search for time series with these attributes.

# Raises

ISNotStored

: Raised if no time series matches the inputs.
  Raised if the inputs match more than one time series.

ISOperationNotAllowed

: Raised if the inputs match more than one time series.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> gen1 = system.get_component(Generator, "gen1")
>>> ts_full = system.get_time_series(gen1, "active_power")
>>> ts_slice = system.get_time_series(
    gen1,
    "active_power",
    start_time=datetime(year=2030, month=1, day=1, hour=5),
    length=5,
)
```

# See Also

list_time_series

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.has_time_series
documentation:
Return True if the component has time series matching the inputs.

Parameters
----------
component : Component
    Component to check for matching time series.
variable_name : str | None
    Optional, search for time series with this name.
time_series_type : Type[TimeSeriesData]
    Optional, search for time series with this type.
user_attributes : str
    Optional, search for time series with these attributes.

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.list_time_series
documentation:
Return all time series that match the inputs.

# Parameters

component

: Component to which the time series must be attached.

variable_name

: Optional, search for time series with this name.

time_series_type

: Optional, search for time series with this type.

start_time

: If not None, take a slice of the time series starting at this time.

length

: If not None, take a slice of the time series with this length.

user_attributes

: Optional, search for time series with these attributes.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> gen1 = system.get_component(Generator, "gen1")
>>> for ts in system.list_time_series(gen1):
    print(ts)
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.list_time_series_metadata
documentation:
Return all time series metadata that match the inputs.

# Parameters

component

: Component to which the time series must be attached.

variable_name

: Optional, search for time series with this name.

time_series_type

: Optional, search for time series with this type.

user_attributes

: Optional, search for time series with these attributes.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> gen1 = system.get_component(Generator, "gen1")
>>> for metadata in system.list_time_series_metadata(gen1):
    print(metadata)
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.remove_time_series
documentation:
Remove all time series arrays attached to the components matching the inputs.

# Parameters

components

: Affected components

variable_name

: Optional, search for time series with this name.

time_series_type

: Optional, search for time series with this type.

user_attributes

: Optional, search for time series with these attributes.

# Raises

ISNotStored

: Raised if no time series match the inputs.

ISOperationNotAllowed

: Raised if the manager was created in read-only mode.

# Examples

```pycon
>>> gen1 = system.get_component(Generator, "gen1")
>>> system.remove_time_series(gen1, "active_power")
```

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.serialize_system_attributes
documentation: Allows subclasses to serialize attributes at the root level.

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.deserialize_system_attributes
documentation:
Allows subclasses to deserialize attributes stored in the JSON at the root level.

The method should modify self with its custom attributes in data.

methods.name: infrasys.system.System.handle_data_format_upgrade
documentation:
Allows subclasses to upgrade data models.

The parameter data contains the full contents of the serialized JSON file.
The method should modify the data models in-place.
